chunks.text: Order Sheet HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P.No.D-2775 OF 2022 Before: Justice Muhammad Saleem Jessar. Justice Nisar Ahmed Bhanbhro. For hearing of MA-6875 of 2024 Petitioner: Through M/s. Atharuddin Abro and Muhammad Rahim Gaju, advocate Respondents: Through M/s. Muhammad Ismail Bhutto Additional A.G Sindh and Mr. Rafique Ahmed Dahri Assistant Advocate General, Sindh a/w Noordin Regional Director LG Mirpurkhas Division o/b Secretary LG Department. Date of hearing: 30.10.2025 Date of Decision: 30.10.2025 ORDER NISAR AHMED BHANBHRO, J;- This petition was disposed of vide order dated 24.10.2023, which reads as under:- “ Arguments heard and record perused. Learned Counsel for the Petitioners states that parawise comments of Respondents are contrary to record as Petitioners were relieved on the basis of a Letter dated 13.07.2022 (At Page-67 Annexure "D"), that is, due to the insufficiency of funds. Learned AAG has rebutted this line of arguments and has referred to Parawise Comments in which it is specifically mentioned that the appointments of the Petitioners were based on fake letters and it was maneuvered by Petitioners in league with some officials. He has referred to subsequent Letter dated 09.09.2022, appended with the Parawise Comments, that the credentials of the Petitioners are doubtful. This Petition is disposed of, inter alia, as the matter is already pending before the authority for verification of the credentials of the Petitioners. In this regard, Secretary Local Government will give a patient hearing to the Petitioners and after considering the record and seeking input from the concerned Department will pass the necessary Orders, preferably within 04(four) weeks from today.

This Petition is disposed of, inter alia, as the matter is already pending before the authority for verification of the credentials of the Petitioners. In this regard, Secretary Local Government will give a patient hearing to the Petitioners and after considering the record and seeking input from the concerned Department will pass the necessary Orders, preferably within 04(four) weeks from today. Petition alongwith pending Application, if any, stands disposed of.” 2. Pursuant to the Court orders, the appointment of the petitioner was examined by the local government department wherein it transpired that the record of four petitioners Zahid Ahmed, Ghulam Qadir, Zahid Hussain and Iqrar Hussain was found available whereas the record of remaining five petitioners namely Javed Ahmed, Naeemullah, Muhammad Salik, Bhadur Ali and Zulfiquar Ali could not be verified. Such report was submitted before this Court through learned Addl. Advocate General Sindh. In the inquiry report it has not been denied specifically that the petitioner were not appointed by the local government. Non verification of the record was due to an inquiry being conducted by NAB where as per the inquiry committee the entire appointment record is available with NAB Karachi. The service of the petitioners with the respondent Town Committee Samaro has not been denied. However, the petitioners were relieved by the Town Officer Town Committee Samaro on the ground of over employment vide notification dated 13.07.2022 and since then petitioners have become shuttlecock in between local government department and the town committee Samaro. 3. Since, in the inquiry report nowhere transpires that the appointment of the petitioners was bogus therefore, the petitioners have got a right to serve in the department. Even no adverse action under Sindh Councils Service Rules 2017 has been taken against the petitioners and the petitioners have served in the respondent- town committee since 2012, at no point of time there appointments were declared to be dubious. 4, In view of above discussion, the respondents are directed to issue fresh posting orders to the petitioners at some suitable place or at the same place of posting they were working in the year 2022.

Since, in the inquiry report nowhere transpires that the appointment of the petitioners was bogus therefore, the petitioners have got a right to serve in the department. Even no adverse action under Sindh Councils Service Rules 2017 has been taken against the petitioners and the petitioners have served in the respondent- town committee since 2012, at no point of time there appointments were declared to be dubious. 4, In view of above discussion, the respondents are directed to issue fresh posting orders to the petitioners at some suitable place or at the same place of posting they were working in the year 2022. The Secretary Local Government shall also decide the period they remained without salary in accordance with the Service Rules, the petitioners shall also be entitled for the payment of the salaries since their relieving from the concerned Town Committee. The needful shall be done within the period of days from the date of receipt of this order. Office is directed to send the copy of this order to the respondents for compliance. Additional Registrar to issue compliance. JUDGE JUDGE Ahmed/Pa,
